title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: Three example VCFs ship package: Strelka SBS file, Strelka indel file, Mutect file (small subsets, GRCh37 / hg19).
code: sbs_file <- system.file(   "extdata", "Strelka-SBS-GRCh37", "Strelka.SBS.GRCh37.s1.vcf",   package = "mSigSpectra" ) id_file <- system.file(   "extdata", "Strelka-ID-GRCh37", "Strelka.ID.GRCh37.s1.vcf",   package = "mSigSpectra" ) mutect_file <- system.file(   "extdata", "Mutect-GRCh37", "Mutect.GRCh37.s1.vcf",   package = "mSigSpectra" )

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: read_vcf() returns data.table whatever columns VCF body contains. pass filter = "PASS" match Strelka convention.
code: sbs_vcf <- read_vcf(sbs_file, filter = "PASS") nrow(sbs_vcf) #> [1] 798 head(sbs_vcf[, c("CHROM", "POS", "REF", "ALT", "FILTER")]) #>     CHROM     POS    REF    ALT FILTER #>    <char>   <int> <char> <char> <char> #> 1:      1  906904      C      A   PASS #> 2:      1 1821952      C      T   PASS #> 3:      1 1963733      G      A   PASS #> 4:      1 2249474      G      T   PASS #> 5:      1 2545983      A      T   PASS #> 6:      1 2738084      C      A   PASS

what: 4. Split into SBS / DBS / ID sub-tables
title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: split_vcf() partitions rows REF/ALT length alone. “pure SBS” VCF expect everything $SBS, Strelka’s SBS caller can emit adjacent SBS pairs indel caller see; mSigSpectra merge DBSs (see “Gotchas” README) — stay SBSs.
code: sbs_split <- split_vcf(sbs_vcf, name_of_vcf = "Strelka.SBS.GRCh37.s1") sapply(sbs_split[c("SBS", "DBS", "ID")], nrow) #> SBS DBS  ID  #> 798   0   0

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: annotate_sbs_or_dbs_vcf() adds: seq.<N>bases — flanking sequence context (default seq.21bases). ref genomes shipped transcript-ranges table (GRCh37 / GRCh38 / GRCm38), transcript-strand columns trans.start.pos, trans.end.pos, trans.strand, trans.Ensembl.gene.ID, trans.gene.symbol, plus bothstrand count (number overlapping transcripts). returns list annotated.vcf (table ) discarded.variants (always NULL SBS / DBS).
code: sbs_ann <- annotate_sbs_or_dbs_vcf(sbs_split$SBS,                                    ref_genome = "GRCh37")$annotated.vcf new_cols <- setdiff(colnames(sbs_ann), colnames(sbs_split$SBS)) new_cols #> [1] "seq.21bases"           "trans.start.pos"       "trans.end.pos"         #> [4] "trans.strand"          "trans.Ensembl.gene.ID" "trans.gene.symbol"     #> [7] "POS2"                  "bothstrand"            "count"

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: Strelka indel VCF goes annotate_id_vcf(), left-justifies indel, extracts local repeat / microhomology context, emits three classification strings (COSMIC_83, Koh_89, Koh_476). Like annotate_sbs_or_dbs_vcf() returns list annotated.vcf + discarded.variants.
code: id_vcf <- read_vcf(id_file, filter = "PASS") id_split <- split_vcf(id_vcf, name_of_vcf = "Strelka.ID.GRCh37.s1") id_ann <- annotate_id_vcf(id_split$ID,                           ref_genome = "GRCh37")$annotated.vcf id_ann[1, c("CHROM", "POS", "REF", "ALT", "COSMIC_83", "Koh_89")] #>     CHROM     POS    REF    ALT COSMIC_83       Koh_89 #>    <char>   <int> <char> <char>    <char>       <char> #> 1:      1 5288645     TG      T DEL:C:1:1 [Del(C):R2]A  cat_id83 <- vcf_to_id_catalog(id_ann, type = "ID83", ref_genome = "GRCh37",                               region = "genome", sample_name = "s1") cat_id89 <- vcf_to_id_catalog(id_ann, type = "ID89", ref_genome = "GRCh37",                               region = "genome", sample_name = "s1") cat_id476 <- vcf_to_id_catalog(id_ann, type = "ID476", ref_genome = "GRCh37",                                region = "genome", sample_name = "s1") plot_ID83(cat_id83, plot_title = "Strelka.ID.GRCh37.s1 — ID83") plot_ID89(cat_id89, plot_title = "Strelka.ID.GRCh37.s1 — ID89") plot_ID476(cat_id476, plot_title = "Strelka.ID.GRCh37.s1 — ID476")

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: Convert raw counts (per category) mutation density (per megabase context) using shipped k-mer abundances:
code: cat96_density <- transform_catalog(cat96,                                    target_counts_or_density = "density") attr(cat96_density, "counts_or_density") #> [1] "density" plot_SBS96(cat96_density,            plot_title = "Strelka.SBS.GRCh37.s1 — SBS96 (density)")

what: 10. Collapse from finer to coarser resolutions
title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
code: cat96_from_1536 <- collapse_catalog(cat1536, to = "SBS96") all.equal(as.numeric(cat96_from_1536[, 1]),           as.numeric(cat96[, 1])) #> [1] TRUE

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: ICAMS-native CSV default output format today; SigProfiler COSMIC formats supported input.
code: out_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "Strelka.SBS.GRCh37.s1.SBS96.csv") write_catalog(cat96, out_path) cat96_back <- read_catalog(out_path, region = "genome") identical(as.numeric(cat96), as.numeric(cat96_back)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Reading VCFs and Building Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs
text: ?read_vcf — caller-agnostic reader. ?annotate_sbs_or_dbs_vcf — sequence context + transcript strand SBS / DBS. ?annotate_id_vcf — indel justification + classification (COSMIC 83 / Koh 89 / Koh 476) + transcript strand. ?vcf_to_sbs_catalog, ?vcf_to_dbs_catalog, ?vcf_to_id_catalog — per-variant-type catalog builders. ?transform_catalog / ?collapse_catalog — counts ↔︎ density finer--coarser collapse. ?check_and_remove_discarded_variants — optional defensive QC. README’s “Gotchas” section documents cases pipeline intentionally leaves user.

what: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: Build mutational-spectrum catalogs VCF files. mSigSpectra lean, caller-agnostic successor ICAMS — keeps numerical core (VCF reading, annotation, indel justification, SBS / DBS / ID catalog construction) drops everything else. Plotting now handled separate mSigPlot package. Shiny, PDF / zip reporting, per-caller VAF extraction intentionally scope.

title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: read_vcf() — reads VCF file body. Caller-agnostic: parse FORMAT / sample columns extract VAF. split_vcf() — partitions VCF SBS / DBS / ID sub-tables REF/ALT length alone. annotate_vcf() — adds flanking sequence context (BSgenome::getSeq), transcript strand (GenomicRanges::findOverlaps shipped GENCODE tables), — indels — left-justifies categorizes (COSMIC-83 / Koh-89 / Koh-476). vcf_to_catalog() — produces single catalog types: SBS96, SBS192, SBS1536, DBS78, DBS136, DBS144, ID83, ID89, ID166, ID476. output plain numeric matrix attributes (type, counts_or_density, ref_genome, region, abundance). S3 classes. Functions key attr(x, "type") via explicit checks.

title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: also need BSgenome package(s) reference genome(s): plotting, install mSigPlot:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("steverozen/mSigSpectra") BiocManager::install(c(   "BSgenome.Hsapiens.1000genomes.hs37d5",   # GRCh37 / hg19   "BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38",            # GRCh38 / hg38   "BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10"            # GRCm38 / mm10 )) remotes::install_github("steverozen/mSigPlot")

title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: See vignettes/mSigSpectra.Rmd full walk-reads real VCF, generates SBS / DBS / ID catalogs, writes disk, plots one mSigPlot.
code: library(mSigSpectra) library(mSigPlot)  vcf <- read_vcf("my_sample.vcf", filter = "PASS") parts <- split_vcf(vcf)  ann_sbs <- annotate_vcf(parts$SBS, ref_genome = "GRCh38",                         variant_type = "SBS") sbs96 <- vcf_to_catalog(ann_sbs, type = "SBS96",                         ref_genome = "GRCh38", region = "genome",                         sample_name = "my_sample")  plot_SBS96(sbs96)

title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: UpperCamelCase gone. S3 catalog classes (SBS96Catalog, IndelCatalog, …) gone — catalogs plain matrices attributes. variant.caller argument per-caller VAF extractors (GetStrelkaVAF, GetMutectVAF, etc.) gone.

what: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: mSigSpectra deliberately stays several judgment calls ICAMS made . reader returns rows VCF actually contains. responsible sanitizing downstream analysis caller / VCF source following quirks:

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
what: 1. Adjacent SBSs are not merged into DBSs
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: callers (notably Strelka) emit real DBS two adjacent SBSs positions p p+1. ICAMS’s Strelka-specific code path tried detect VAF similarity merged single DBS row. mSigSpectra never — result one fewer DBS two extra SBSs per pair. VCFs come caller need DBSs, merge adjacent SBSs (using VAF column) calling split_vcf().

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: Tri-allelic tetra-allelic sites can encoded single VCF row multiple comma-separated alleles ALT (→ G,T), multiple rows (CHROM, POS), bcftools norm -m - “split” representation. mSigSpectra: Multi-allelic--one-row rows (ALT contains ,) — read_vcf() passes . split_vcf() classify SBS / DBS / ID since nchar(ALT) 3+. land silently split_vcf()$discarded. want include , split alternate allele row first (e.g. bcftools norm   -m -). -position multi-row rows kept read_vcf(). optional check_and_remove_discarded_variants() discard reason “Variant CHROM, POS, REF different ALT” — call explicitly want behavior.

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: complex change like REF = "AG", ALT = "TC" (two-base substitution neither base matches) can equally well represented two adjacent SBSs (→ T G → C), single DBS, one deletion plus one insertion. community standard. mSigSpectra: REF[1] != ALT[1] lengths differ — discarded check_and_remove_discarded_variants() “Complex indel”; left place bare read_vcf() + split_vcf() path (nchar(REF) != nchar(ALT) flags ID even though conform ICAMS / COSMIC indel convention shared first base). 3-bp+ -length substitutions like ACT → TGA — discarded check_and_remove_discarded_variants() “Variant involves three nucleotides”; classified split_vcf()$discarded split_vcf() path. curated convention , apply catalog construction.

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: callers emit REF = "TA", ALT = "TT" (T position 1 unchanged). degenerate DBSs — really single-base substitutions disguise. check_and_remove_discarded_variants() discards “Wrong DBS variant”; split_vcf() classify DBS unless filter first.

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: Variants non-{,C,G,T} REF bases (e.g. N) canonicalized. check_and_remove_discarded_variants() removes ; vcf_to_catalog() ultimately drops pentanucleotide context contains N warns.

previous_headings: Gotchas you have to handle yourself
title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: default read_vcf(filter = TRUE) keeps rows FILTER {"PASS", ".", ""} — union common caller defaults. bit-exact compatible single caller — Strelka Mutect keep "PASS", Freebayes uses ".". need parity one specific caller, pass filter = "PASS" (caller’s exact status string) explicitly.

code: vcf <- read_vcf(file, filter = "PASS") clean <- check_and_remove_discarded_variants(vcf, name_of_vcf = file) parts <- split_vcf(clean$df, name_of_vcf = file) # Inspect clean$discarded.variants and parts$discarded for anything you # want to recover or re-encode before the annotate / catalog step.

title: Build Mutational-Spectrum Catalogs from VCF Files
text: Version 0.0.1. R CMD check: 0 errors, 0 warnings. 235 testthat assertions across 13 test files. SBS96 catalog construction bit-exact ICAMS Mutect VCFs; diverges ICAMS’s Strelka path documented -adjacency-merge design choice. DBS catalog construction passes structural / shape tests; full numeric parity vs ICAMS yet verified (planned). ID83 / ID89 / ID476 catalogs wired end--end real Strelka ID VCFs. transform_catalog() (counts ↔︎ density) collapse_catalog() (SBS1536 → SBS96, SBS192 → SBS96, DBS144 → DBS78) implemented. read_catalog() / write_catalog() round-trip ICAMS-native CSV losslessly; SigProfiler input formats parse SBS96, SBS1536, ID83.

title: Annotate an in-memory ID (indel) VCF with sequence context, transcript strand, and COSMIC / Koh indel categories — annotate_id_vcf

what: Read a mutational-spectrum catalog from a file — read_catalog
title: Read a mutational-spectrum catalog from a file — read_catalog
text: Auto-detects file format first row. Recognized formats: ICAMS native CSV — first N columns header columns (mutation type, context, etc), remaining columns per-sample counts / densities. SigProfiler TSV / CSV — single header column bracketed mutation labels like [C>], AA[C>]AA, PCAWG indel codes like 1:Del:C:0. COSMIC CSV — SBS96 uses ICAMS-external row-header format; SBS192 uses stranded variant ; DBS78 / ID83 also share SigProfiler-style layout.
